$comment: Full scored catalog as site roster — derived from docs/research/2026-07-03-ds-catalog/catalog.json. Scores are recon-level prioritization evidence (0-10 per domain, general 0-100), not published verdicts.

systems.name: Adobe Spectrum 2
systems.note: Contrast-solved Leonardo color with true 3-tier tokens; dimension/component-height/font tokens across desktop+mobile platform scales. React Aria + Spectrum Web Components + CSS give 50+ production components with reference-grade engineered a11y; 959 workflow icons; tokens shipped as JSON-Schema-validated design data plus MCP server.

systems.name: Google Material 3
systems.note: HCT runtime dynamic color plus complete type-scale-token roles (display/headline/title/body/label) and 4dp/density layout. Spring-based Expressive + Standard motion schemes with duration/easing tokens and choreography is the strongest motion language scored; Material Symbols variable font (2500+ glyphs, 4 axes, 3 styles) is the icon gold standard. 30+ components across Compose/Web/Flutter; theme builder and color-utilities open in 6 ports.

systems.name: IBM Carbon
systems.note: Industry-defining open system: ~235 semantic tokens over --cds-* with 4 built-in themes, and dedicated packages for every foundation — @carbon/type (IBM Plex + tokenized scale), @carbon/layout (spacing scale), @carbon/motion (productive/expressive with standard/entrance/exit easing + duration tokens), @carbon/grid, @carbon/icons (2000+ glyph library). 50+ production React components with automated a11y checking (achecker) and exemplary public docs.

systems.name: Microsoft Fluent 2
systems.note: Open Lab/LCH curve-based ramp generator powers a public Theme Designer with a global->alias split (~180 alias color tokens); tokens cover spacing, type, radius, stroke, and motion (durations + four named curves + choreography guidance). Own Segoe UI Variable typeface with platform type ramps, a large open Fluent icon system (Regular/Filled), and 50+ components across Web/iOS/Android/Windows. Industry-defining breadth; a11y is documented WCAG practice rather than by-construction.

systems.name: GitHub Primer
systems.note: Three-tier Style Dictionary pipeline (base->functional->component) with an HSLuv Prism authoring tool and automated WCAG audits over ~100 token pairs per theme, plus an org.primer.llm machine-readable extension - accessibility as engineering, not claims. Three-tier typography tokens, Octicons (600+ icons, multi-size 12/16/24/48px), and 89 components (React + Rails). Motion tokens exist but are the thinnest layer.

systems.name: Ant Design
systems.note: Three-tier seed->map->alias token system spans color (parametric HSV ramps), typography, spacing (sizeStep/sizeUnit), motion (motionDuration/motionEase), radius, shadow and breakpoints - one of the most complete algorithmic non-color token architectures. ~72 MIT-licensed components with own @ant-design/icons set; motion is principle-led with a companion Ant Motion library rather than deep choreography tokens. A11y is guidance-level, not contrast-by-construction.

systems.name: Twilio Paste
systems.note: 100+ components on a two-tier token system (aliases -> semantic) with an explicit Theme + Customization provider layer, Figma variable modes, and full box-shadow/radii/spacing/z-index/font token categories. Exemplary accessibility, inclusive-design and content docs; first-party icon set with guidelines; motion is the weak spot (limited/lightly-surfaced animation tokens). Fully open source with public Figma and token packages.

systems.name: React Aria Components
systems.note: 50+ fully headless components with the deepest a11y engineering of any primitive set — W3C ARIA APG, 30+ locales, 13 calendar and 5 numbering systems, RTL, exhaustive screen-reader testing — plus first-class entry/exit animation hooks (data-entering/exiting, isExiting, Motion integration). Intentionally style-free: no color/type/spacing/icons of its own (that is Spectrum's layer), so those domains score near zero by design.

systems.name: Cloudscape Design System
systems.note: AWS's open-sourced console system: real Sass/JS/JSON design-token package with CTI naming, themeable tokens, comfortable/compact density, and light/dark visual modes; full Open Sans type scale (heading/body/code/display roles), a complete motion language (3 duration tokens + 3 named easing curves + scale/fade/slide choreography + reduced-motion), 130+ tested/accessible components including charts, and extensive pattern guidelines. Exemplary public docs.

systems.name: Radix Colors + Themes
systems.note: 12-step role semantics with APCA text-contrast by construction (Lc 60/90) and P3 via color-gamut. Themes adds a 9-step type scale (size+line-height+letter-spacing), 9-step spacing scale with scaling factor and layout engine, and ~35 components on WAI-ARIA-APG primitives. Motion is left to CSS/Framer with no token language; Radix Icons is a small first-party 15x15 set; MIT source public but the color generator is not.

systems.name: Shopify Polaris
systems.note: Algorithmic HSLuv color factory purpose-built for cross-hue WCAG contrast parity, exposed as two-tier primitive->semantic --p- CSS vars covering color, space, and motion (duration-100..500 + linear/ease easings). Own 400+ commerce-focused icon library, purpose-built UI type scale with tabular numerics, and exemplary docs with strong design rationale. React implementation is now deprecated in favor of open web components (maintenance mode), but everything stays public and extractable.

systems.name: Meta Astryx
systems.note: MIT-licensed (facebook/astryx), 150+ accessible React components on StyleX compile-time CSS with a declarative token config (color/typography/motion/spacing) and 10 swappable themes; light-dark() dual-mode semantic tokens, CLI + MCP server for agents. A11y is 'accessible defaults' rather than contrast-by-construction, and no confirmed first-party icon set; beta, so foundation docs still thin.

systems.name: Vercel Geist
systems.note: design.md machine-readable full-system spec: type roles 72-14px, 4px spacing scale, 100-1000 accent scales in sRGB+P3 oklch, own dev-tools icon set, ~40 React components (@vercel/geist). High-contrast accessible color claim but no contrast-by-construction; motion is documented prose (cubic-bezier) not tokens. Exemplary public docs, docs-only (components not open-source).

systems.name: GOV.UK Design System
systems.note: Accessibility exemplar: WCAG 2.2 AA baked into every component with rigorous public rationale (APCA proposed in govuk-frontend #4066 but unshipped). Hand-authored functional-colour + named Sass palette with govuk-colour/govuk-functional-colour functions — a real semantic layer but no generation algorithm, no dark mode. Tokenized responsive 6-point type scale (New Transport) on a 5px vertical rhythm and a 9-unit responsive spacing scale. ~35-43 components, all accessibility-hardened. No motion language and no first-party icon system.

systems.name: SAP Fiori (Horizon)
systems.note: Benchmark token architecture: Horizon base.less carries ~3930 @sap params with contrast-by-construction generation (contrast()/darken()/lighten() LESS functions resolving text colors against backgrounds), a formal reference->semantic->component tier model exported as a W3C-style design-tokens.json (1538 vars, typed, described), and first-class HCB/HCW high-contrast theme variants. Ships the bespoke '72' typeface family and SAP icon fonts; Apache-2.0 open. Motion/components live in the separate UI5 libraries, not this theming base - hence low component/motion here.

systems.name: Pinterest Gestalt
systems.note: Long-running open React system: 85 code-backed components across Web/iOS/Android, semantic color tokens fully migrated to variables for effortless light/dark modes, plus typography/spacing/elevation tokens and a 24px-grid first-party icon set. Standout engineering-first tooling (codemods on every breaking change, ESLint plugin, VS Code extension) and deep WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility foundation; motion is guidance-led (useReducedMotion) rather than a full tokenized motion language.

systems.name: Elastic UI Framework (EUI)
systems.note: Battle-tested system powering Kibana: Emotion useEuiTheme() with computed light+dark themes, colorblind data-vis palettes (euiPaletteColorBlind, behind-text variants) and makeHighContrastColor contrast helpers, and systematic spacing/typography/border/breakpoint plus animation tokens (speeds, easings, euiCanAnimate reduced-motion). 50+ components including a production data grid, own EuiIcon set, accessibility-first. Open source but under Elastic/SSPL licensing rather than a permissive license.

systems.name: Semi Design
systems.note: The most token-rich, theming-forward system in the set: 3000+ design tokens, a live theme editor + DSM, multiple built-in brand themes (Feishu, Douyin, CapCut) and one-click Figma-to-code. 60-80+ components on a Foundation/Adapter architecture with full W3C a11y (keyboard, focus, ARIA); open source with extensive live-demo docs.

systems.name: USWDS
systems.note: Grades regularized to relative-luminance ranges so grade difference alone guarantees WCAG/Section 508 contrast (24 families x 10 grades); accessibility anchor 10. Public Sans own typeface with combined font-family+size tokens and a 100-900 weight scale, systematic spacing units, and 40+ components each with UX/a11y/impl guidance. Ships Material + usa + uswds icon sets (leans on third-party Material); no real motion token language; fully open source and tokens.

systems.name: Porsche Design System
systems.org: Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG
systems.note: Rare fully open-source OEM brand system: 60+ WCAG 2.2 AA web components with SSR and first-class React/Angular/Vue/Next/Remix wrappers. Broad token set (color/spacing/typography/motion/shadow/border/blur/gradient/breakpoint) using light-dark() for automatic theming, motion duration/easing tokens, own icon library and Porsche Next typeface, plus Tailwind/SCSS/Emotion/vanilla-extract styling paths.

systems.name: Helios Design System
systems.note: Rare Ember-first (plus React) open-source system with a clean three-tier token architecture (primitive/semantic/component, including per-product brand tokens), motion tokens (durations + cubic-bezier easing), and elevation. 56+ components with strong states/a11y (WCAG 2.2 AA by construction), first-party Flight icons (outlined/filled/off/contained at 16/24px), and exemplary docs with content/voice guidelines. Typography leans on system fonts rather than an engineered typeface.

systems.name: Salesforce Lightning (SLDS)
systems.note: Literal origin of the term 'design tokens'. SLDS 2 global styling hooks (--slds-g-*) + 1-100 token ramps; ~33 components as accessible framework-independent blueprints (W3C/ARIA keyboard behavior). Own multi-category icon set (action/custom/doctype/standard/utility), Kinetics motion system in rollout. Source public on GitHub but token-generation tooling not open.

systems.name: Atlassian Design System
systems.note: Deep, well-named token architecture (~180 color tokens across neutrals/6 semantic roles/10 accent ramps/chart, 8px density-responsive spacing, own Atlassian Sans/Mono typefaces, 8 duration + 5 easing motion tokens, first-party @atlaskit/icon system with guidelines). Palette generation is hand-picked hex with no contrast-solved algorithm, and JS-rendered docs resist inspection; source is mirrored to GitHub under Apache-2.0 but the internal repo is the source of truth.

systems.name: Material UI (MUI)
systems.note: Most-used React library: 50+ production components with variants/states, MIT-licensed, mature theme object (palette light/main/dark, typography, 8px spacing, breakpoints, and real transitions duration/easing tokens). Material Icons ship a large first-party set. Color/token system is solid but its default palette is a curated Material implementation rather than a contrast-solved generation engine, and a11y is decent-practice rather than guaranteed-by-construction.

systems.name: Pajamas Design System
systems.note: Fully-open, handbook-integrated system: token authoring/reading/usage guides, 50+ Vue components via GitLab UI, first-party static+animated icon set, and a real motion language (4 duration tokens, 3 easing curves, choreography + prefers-reduced-motion). Strong a11y with conformance reporting; one of the most transparent systems (repo, community Figma, Storybook all public).

systems.name: Backpack
systems.note: Very actively released (v43+, 345+ releases), Apache-2.0 multi-platform (web/iOS/Android) system with 70+ web components, a separate bpk-foundations tokens package, a first-party bpk-svgs icon set, and animation utilities. Documented accessibility/contrast-driven token consolidation work; fully open source and design data public.

systems.name: Salt Design System
systems.note: Enterprise system from J.P. Morgan with a genuine three-tier token architecture (Foundation to Palette to Characteristic), 4 density modes on a 4px grid, and ~80 accessible React components (core + lab) tested with NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver to WCAG 2.1 AA. Color is a curated brand palette with real semantic layering but no generation/contrast-by-construction; motion is limited to a basic duration foundation. Apache-2.0 source plus public Figma community libraries.

systems.name: shadcn/ui
systems.note: Static borrowed color scales, no generation or contrast strategy (color anchor 3); real system is the semantic CSS-variable convention + --radius derivation. 80+ components built on Radix primitives giving genuine WAI-ARIA a11y, keyboard/focus/screen-reader. Lucide default icons (also Tabler/HugeIcons), tailwindcss-animate + data-state motion. Fully open, enormous ecosystem influence.

systems.name: Workday Canvas
systems.note: Mature 3-tier token architecture (base/system/brand) in a dedicated @workday/canvas-tokens-web package with semantic naming and tenant brand theming; ~50 production React components (120 stories) with a formal accessibility-review gate, first-party Workday icon library, and motion tokens. Apache-2.0, actively maintained TS/React Canvas Kit with a disciplined three-version support model.

systems.name: Apple Human Interface Guidelines
systems.note: Guidelines-only with no public token repo, but the most influential cross-platform design language: engineered typefaces (SF Pro/SF Mono, Dynamic Type), SF Symbols (6000+ configurable, weight/scale variants) as a genuine own icon system, deep motion/materials and accessibility guidance. Scores are capped by being docs-only (no extractable code or design data) rather than by quality.

systems.name: TDesign
systems.note: Cross-framework (Vue2/3, React, Flutter, MiniProgram) tokens via tdesign-common, all colors as CSS variables with paired light/dark palettes and a public Theme Generator that derives brand-based light+dark themes; ~2350 first-party icons and 60+ production components per framework. Real semantic+palette token layer but no contrast-by-construction, so short of Material/Radix depth.

systems.name: Base UI
systems.note: 40+ unstyled accessible React primitives (Combobox, Autocomplete, Menubar, Navigation Menu, Select, Slider, Dialog, Toast, etc.) with deep a11y engineering: WAI-ARIA APG conformance, focus management (initialFocus/finalFocus), keyboard nav, broad screen-reader testing. Stable v1.0 (Dec 2025), the Radix Primitives successor from the Radix/MUI/Floating UI team. Deliberately ships no visual tokens — no color, type, motion, or icons.

systems.name: LeafyGreen
systems.note: Mature open devtools system: 80+ individually-versioned @leafygreen-ui packages with dedicated tokens (spacing, typeScales, fontFamilies/weights, transitionDuration), palette, typography, icons, and a11y packages; documented WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 text, 3:1 non-text) and dark mode. Exemplary monorepo/versioning model; motion limited to duration tokens, no OKLCH/contrast-generation.

systems.name: Garden
systems.note: Long-running (v9) open-source React system with a standout color system: 12-shade ramps engineered for consistent contrast (600=3:1, 700=4.5:1, 800+=7:1), primitive + semantic tokens on an App→Group→Element→Modifier→State taxonomy, and full dark-mode logic. ~50 components, first-party svg-icons (stroke/fill, 12/16/32px), enforced 4.5:1 contrast and no-color-alone accessibility, and docs that deliberately blur design/content/engineering. Contrast-consistent ramps but no generative engine; modest motion foundations.

systems.name: Braid Design System
systems.note: Themeable multi-brand system on vanilla-extract with CSS-variable themes (seekJobs, seekBusiness, wireframe) and a tones system; a genuine reference for multi-brand tokens. Standout layout grammar (Box/Stack/Columns/Inline) across 70+ accessible React components, plus Playroom live editing. Fully open source with excellent docs; motion is minimal and icons are a modest first-party set.

systems.name: Coinbase Design System (CDS)
systems.note: Rare fully-open cross-platform (React + React Native) fintech system: two-tier semantic-token + raw-spectrum color (11+ hues, auto light/dark), numeric 8px spacing scale, full typographic role scale (display/title/headline/body/label/caption), radii/elevation, breakpoints, first-party cds-icons, Lottie motion, WCAG AA guidance. No OKLCH or contrast-by-construction; motion is Lottie-driven rather than tokenized.

systems.name: Chakra UI v3
systems.note: Panda-derived semantic-token shape (contrast/fg/subtle/muted/emphasized/solid/focusRing per palette) with colorPalette CSS-variable indirection is a genuinely clean re-theming pattern, though palettes are still hand-picked hex with no contrast-solved generation. Standout is breadth: 130+ components on the Ark UI headless/a11y foundation, plus a real keyframe/duration animation token layer. No first-party icon set; fully open source.

systems.name: HeroUI
systems.note: v3 ground-up rewrite (Tailwind v4, React Aria Components, WCAG 2.1 AA). Notable color architecture-reversal: v2->v3 dropped shade scales + computed contrast for flat OKLCH semantic roles with color-mix() state derivation (4/8/14% surface blends, 15% soft variants, per-theme shadow/dark definitions). Native CSS motion with global data-reduce-motion + prefers-reduced-motion. 75+ web components. Typography deliberately minimal — removed type vars, delegates to Tailwind utilities. Uses third-party icons (Gravity/Heroicons in examples), no first-party set.

systems.name: PrimeReact
systems.note: Notable 3-tier token architecture (primitive/semantic/component) with swappable presets (Aura, Material, Lara, Nora), scoped-token API, and base-vs-preset split feeding CSS variables; 90+ components including data tables/charts/tree, WCAG 2.1 AA with per-component a11y docs, first-party PrimeIcons, media-query/class dark mode. Open source.

systems.name: Toss Design System (TDS)
systems.note: Standout is own typeface engineering: Toss Product Sans, purpose-built for Korean hangul + financial contexts with tabular/variable numeral modes, plus Tossface (3500+ emoji). Full color families (50-900) with semantic background tokens, documented motion tokens (instant/fast/standard/slow/page with named easings incl. a spring reserved for success) that collapse under prefers-reduced-motion, 8px spacing scale, 40+ components with useDialog/useToast/useBottomSheet overlay hooks. Public docs but no source repo.

systems.name: Ark UI
systems.note: 45+ WAI-ARIA headless components with identical APIs across React, Solid, Vue, and Svelte, all driven by one Zag.js state-machine definition per component so fixes propagate to every framework at once (Menu, Combobox, DatePicker, Color Picker, Carousel, Tour, etc.). Completely unstyled — no color/typography/spacing/motion/icon tokens; MIT open source, maintained by the Chakra team.

systems.name: Stripe
systems.note: Canonical CIELAB contrast-by-construction color methodology (2019 'Designing accessible color systems') where any two colors 5 levels apart are guaranteed AA — genuine accessibility-by-construction; 284 color custom properties extracted from shipped dashboard CSS with numeric ramps plus a composed semantic layer. Distinctive weight-300 display type on a 4/8pt grid. Closed system; public docs are only stripe-apps, no full component reference.

systems.name: Uber Base
systems.note: baseweb is broad and mature (~70 components, ~9k stars, active) with the strongest non-color story of this batch: 17 animation-duration tokens plus named semantic easing curves with usage guidance, custom UberMove typeface family and a full typography scale, plus sizing/grid/borders/lighting/breakpoints and light/dark/move themes. Color is the weak axis: hand-curated hex primitives with no OKLCH/contrast generation, and Styletron CSS-in-JS theming (no CSS variables). Public docs cover accessibility, motion, and icon design guidelines.

systems.name: NHS design system
systems.note: GOV.UK-Frontend-derived, MIT, SCSS/BEM/Nunjucks with a React port; 47 well-built form/content/nav components, 18-colour palette with a real semantic Sass token layer (text/link/focus/error/button), NHS Frutiger typeface + scale, and WCAG 2.2 AA-targeting-AAA accessibility. Static curated palette (no generation) and near-zero motion language keep color/motion low.

systems.name: GC Design System
systems.note: Government of Canada federal system as Stencil web components with a dedicated three-tier token package (global/base/component, --gcds- prefixed) and components built to guarantee WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast by construction. 42 components incl. an Icon component, bilingual by design, v1.0 stable with a Figma token library and React/Angular wrappers, fully open source. No motion token layer.

systems.name: Linear
systems.note: Closed but extractable: themes generated in LCH from 3 variables (base/accent/contrast) driving ~98 vars, with a shipped semantic CSS layer (--color-bg-level-*, text-primary..quaternary, motion/z/radius/shadow tokens). Refined Inter-based type with cv01/ss03/zero feature settings and tuned tracking. No public component library, icon set, or reference docs — a brilliant but narrow, closed system.

systems.name: Blueprint
systems.note: React toolkit for data-dense desktop apps: documented color variables and Sass-variable tokens on a 4px spacing grid, dark mode, plus a scalable spreadsheet-like Table and Select/Datetime packages. Own icon system (500+ SVG + icon-font). Apache 2.0 open. Typography leans on HTML tags rather than a rich role system; tokens are Sass-var driven, not multi-tier.

systems.name: NL Design System
systems.note: An architecture and community rather than a monolith: a genuine three-tier token hierarchy (brand/common/component) that lets 8+ Dutch municipalities and agencies (Utrecht, Den Haag, Amsterdam, RVO, Logius) theme shared, accessibility-vetted components to their own identity — a rare working multi-brand token model. Fully open (EUPL/CC0). Component maturity and coverage vary by member system, and there is little motion/icon language at the shared layer.

systems.name: Panda CSS
systems.note: Two-tier W3C token engine (raw + conditional semantic) spanning 21 categories incl. colors, sizes/spacing, full typography, radii, shadows, animations/durations/easings; preset-panda ships a Tailwind-derived palette, keyframes, breakpoints and textStyles. It's a styling foundation, not a component/color-generation system: no components, no contrast-solving generation, no icons, no a11y layer.

systems.name: Mantine
systems.note: Open chroma-js color generator with fixed HSL maps whose own docs admit light-hue failure modes, plus off-by-default WCAG-luminance autoContrast; rem-based sizing and a clean xs-xl type/spacing scale but no own typeface. Broad 120+ component library with strong docs; motion is a set of named transition presets (fade/scale/pop/slide) configured per-use rather than a real duration/easing token system, and it leans on third-party Tabler icons. Fully open source.

systems.name: Arco Design
systems.note: Enterprise React library, 60+ components, MIT, actively released (146 releases, latest May 2026). Deep token system driven by Design Lab; palette-dark.js computes dark ramps with hue-band-dependent piecewise logic (deeper than Ant's dark map). First-party IconBox icon library and animation tokens exist. Weaknesses: color package stale (v0.4.0, 2024), docs are JS-rendered and flaky/hard to fetch, no React Aria — accessibility is self-rolled and less rigorous than GOV.UK or HeroUI.

systems.name: Orbit
systems.note: priorRecon was stale: the repo is active (not archived, ~1.4k stars, pushed 2026-01) with 76 React components and a real two-tier token dictionary (foundation + global: space, typography roles, duration/motion, elevations, radius, breakpoints, z-index) plus a Tailwind preset. WCAG A/AA contrast guidance, SkipLink/SkipNavigation a11y components, first-party icon set, MIT open source.

systems.name: gov.br Design System
systems.note: Brazil's federal standard: framework-agnostic core CSS + tokens plus React/Angular libraries, CC0/public-domain on GitLab, actively versioned (v3/v4, 34 releases). Semantic design tokens for color/typography/spacing, Rawline typeface on a Minor Third scale, broad component set, and WCAG/eMAG accessibility. Icons are third-party Font Awesome 6 (icons=3); curated ramps with a semantic layer but no contrast-solved generation.

systems.name: Saga Design System
systems.note: Dark-first, dashboard-oriented token system synced via Token Studio/Figma variables (color, typography, layout, surface, icons) backing grafana/ui (AGPL-3.0). Solid component set and a WCAG 2.1 AA a11y styleguide (partial conformance). Docs repo archived June 2026 and migrated to Storybook; token structure was mid-rebuild, and motion guidance is thin.

systems.name: Odyssey Design System
systems.note: Apache-2.0, MUI-based React system with a dedicated @okta/odyssey-design-tokens package and standout accessibility engineering (WCAG 2.2 AA + WAI-ARIA, contrast/shape/screen-reader built in). Actively maintained (73 releases). Docs site is auth-gated behind Supernova, which weakens public docs and stable-URL access; a strong token + theming/a11y study more than a broad showcase.

systems.name: Nord (Provet)
systems.note: Mature closed system: 71 web components, a full multi-category token set (90+ color tokens, typography with 4 weights, 6 spacings, 6 shadows, radius/z-index) and first-party Nordicons. Motion is thin (3 transition tokens). Source is not public (npm ships compiled output), usage is Nordhealth-restricted, and WCAG 2.1 AA is aspirational rather than engineered.

systems.name: Bootstrap
systems.note: Foundational Sass-maps + compiled CSS-custom-property token system with data-bs-theme light/dark and custom color modes; ~24 components, prefers-reduced-motion-aware transitions, own Bootstrap Icons set (separate package), solid a11y docs. Fully open (MIT). Broad but shallow tokenization; no density modes or systematic contrast generation.

systems.name: daisyUI
systems.note: Fully tokenized Tailwind layer: ~35 CSS-variable themes with semantic color-pair tokens whose contrast is auto-tested on every theme change (contrast-by-construction if you use the correct bg/content pairs), plus v5 global size/radius/border tokens (--size-field, --radius-box). ~50+ semantic components on native HTML with prefers-reduced-motion support, but no first-party icon set and only a light type scale.

systems.name: Adobe Leonardo
systems.note: Contrast-ratio-driven adaptive color engine that inverts the usual workflow — you set target contrast ratios and it generates the swatches to hit them (contrast-by-construction, not checked after), on perceptual color spaces via D3-color/CIECAM02, with adaptive dark-mode themes and colorblind-safe analysis. Produces Spectrum's colors; Apache 2.0, fully open (@adobe/leonardo-contrast-colors + the leonardocolor.io UI). Pure color/a11y tool — no components, type, spacing, motion, or icons.

systems.name: Tailwind palette
systems.note: 24-26 color families x 11 steps, expert-curated (explicitly not algorithmic), v4 hex->OKLCH migration preserving cross-hue balance with P3 wide-gamut targeting. No semantic layer, no documented contrast strategy. Pure palette: not a component/typography/motion/icon system. Fully open, excellent reference docs. Worth studying as the definitive curated ramp set.

systems.name: Scale
systems.note: MPL-2.0 open-source Stencil web components with React/Vue/Angular wrappers and a dedicated @telekom/design-tokens package (CSS output, color/typography/spacing/motion). Notable open-vs-brand split: neutral build is fully open while magenta/typeface stay protected. Solid mid-tier depth and Storybook docs, but token architecture and a11y guarantees are less deep than the top tier.

systems.name: GEL (Global Experience Language)
systems.note: Framework-agnostic, accessibility-first pattern library with code-centric foundations (Reith typeface, grid system) and 20+ ES5 progressive-enhancement reference components explicitly 'not a library.' Exemplary a11y engineering (BBC guidelines + ACT test scripts) but no color-token architecture or motion system; public under Open Government Licence.

systems.name: StyleX
systems.note: Typed themes (defineVars/createTheme) compiling to collision-free atomic CSS with media-query-varying vars; battle-tested at Meta scale and adopted by Figma/Snowflake. Ships zero default tokens itself — a pure mechanism — though it pairs with Open Props (@stylexjs/open-props: OKLCH colors, sizes, easings, animations, fonts). No components, icons, or accessibility layer.

systems.name: vanilla-extract
systems.note: The canonical compile-time token model: createTheme/createThemeContract give fully typed theme contracts enforced by TypeScript, with hierarchical token objects (color, font, space) compiled to scoped CSS vars, zero runtime. Powers Seek's Braid. Purely a mechanism — ships no default palette, tokens, components, icons, motion, or a11y; strength is the type-safe contract pattern itself.

systems.name: Clarity Design System
systems.note: Early standards-based web-components system (@cds/core) with Angular/React shims, design tokens for color/spacing/typography/layout, 200+ first-party icons, own @cds/city typeface, and strong WCAG 2.1 AA rigor (dedicated a11y team, published VPAT). Notably split across repos with Clarity Core largely frozen/deprecated and momentum on the Angular side; motion is thin.

systems.name: Open Props
systems.note: Pure CSS-variable token toolkit: dual color generation (composited palettes vs parametric single-hue OKLCH curve, ~234 color props across 18 families) but no semantic layer and no contrast tooling. Deep size/spacing scales (static + fluid + relative) and a best-in-class motion layer (Penner easings, spring/elastic/bounce, named animation props). No components (only optional buttons.css/normalize) and icons are WIP; no accessibility guarantees. Fully open source and worth studying for its token generation, not as a product design system.

systems.name: Source
systems.note: Editorial React library (source-foundations + source-react-components, Apache-2.0, now in guardian/csnx) with color mapped to journalism pillars, the Guardian superfamily type scale reworked to 9 sizes, and a 4px space scale via foundation functions. Accessible core components but a modest set (form/button/link-centric) with no dedicated motion or icon system.

systems.name: Vant
systems.note: 80+ lightweight mobile Vue components with 700+ CSS-variable style tokens overridable via ConfigProvider (theme-vars / theme-vars-dark / theme-vars-light) and one-switch dark mode plus a first-party @vant/icons set. Theming is CSS-var override rather than a deep semantic-token tier; motion is per-component transitions with no motion language, and no systematic a11y engineering.

systems.name: Style Dictionary
systems.note: The de-facto design-token build system — transforms one JSON source into iOS, Android, CSS, JS, HTML and more, and is DTCG-spec aligned. It ships no tokens, colors, or type of its own (users supply the data), so every visual/a11y domain scores near zero; its value is the pipeline, not a design language. Apache 2.0, fully open, exemplary reference docs.

systems.name: LINE Design System
systems.note: Public LDSM guidelines: Forest Green identity plus ~30 curated core colors, OS-system-font typography with defined type/scale/roles, a components library defining structure/usage/type/pattern, and locale-aware typography treated as first-class (JP/KR/TH/ID). Docs-only with no public source or extractable tokens; a rare fully-public super-app system but guideline-depth rather than an engineered token pipeline.

systems.name: Park UI
systems.note: A curation layer, confirmed: color science is Radix's, vendored wholesale (`import * as radixColors from '@radix-ui/colors'` in generate-colors.ts) with a semantic-token layer generated over it; token mechanics are Panda's. ~63 components are Ark UI primitives (so real a11y, states, and motion presets), with a first-party Panda preset (text-styles, animation-styles, keyframes, durations, shadows, z-index recipes). Icons are third-party (Lucide). MIT open source, now under the chakra-ui org.

systems.name: Untitled UI
systems.note: Verified real 3-layer token architecture in theme.css: raw ramps -> utility color aliases -> semantic text/bg/fg/border roles, plus display type scale and a full shadow scale. But all plain rgb(), zero generation methodology, no contrast-by-construction. Broad React Aria component library (charts, table, date-picker, combobox, file-upload) gives genuine a11y mechanics; MIT open.

systems.name: Kobalte
systems.note: Headless SolidJS primitive library (~50 components) shipping zero styles/tokens/icons; its value is WAI-ARIA-compliant behavior, focus/keyboard/ARIA handling, and an enter/exit animation guide. No visual design layer by design, so token domains score near zero.

systems.name: Melt UI
systems.note: Lowest-level Svelte headless builder API (40+ builders attaching behavior to any element) with zero styles/tokens/icons; strong WAI-ARIA pattern coverage, per-builder a11y docs, and a transitions guide. No visual design system, as intended for a primitive layer.

systems.name: Once UI
systems.note: priorRecon confirmed: 19 hardcoded hex palettes swapped via data-brand attributes, no OKLCH, no contrast code. Redeeming depth is elsewhere: CSS-variable typography with per-family responsive scaling multipliers, spacing/radius tokens, and 100+ open MIT components (forms, charts, modules like command palette), but motion is ad hoc and icons are third-party.

systems.name: Tailwind Catalyst
systems.note: Paywalled Headless UI component scaffolding (24 components: Button, Dialog, Table, Combobox, etc.) built entirely on Tailwind's default theme - explicitly adds no token layer of its own ('relies on the default spacing scale, color palette, shadow scale'). Headless UI gives solid a11y; motion lib + Heroicons bundled. Docs public, source behind $149 Tailwind Plus license.

systems.name: Airbnb DLS
systems.note: Effectively closed: no public repo, no docs/tokens portal, component implementations explicitly internal. Only philosophy pieces (Building a Visual Language), talks, and community reconstructions are public; token examples (color-rausch #ff5a5f, Cereal typeface) are illustrative not canonical. Historically influential (DLS primitives/components/elements model) but nothing systematic is extractable — catalog/study-of-influence value only. Openness anchor 0.
